record.field: Grafana Loki is a horizontally scalable, cost-efficient log aggregation system that indexes only labels and pairs natively with Grafana to explore your logs.

Grafana Loki is a horizontally scalable, cost-efficient log aggregation system that indexes only labels and pairs natively with Grafana to explore your logs.

Inspired by Prometheus, Loki centralizes logs from containers and servers without indexing the full contents of every line: it only indexes a small set of labels and stores the compressed log streams as chunks. That keeps storage and memory use far lower than a full-text search stack like Elasticsearch, making it a practical logging backend for small teams and large fleets alike. It is designed to sit next to Prometheus metrics inside Grafana, so you can pivot from a spike on a dashboard straight to the matching logs.

Key features
  • Indexes only labels and metadata, not full log text — dramatically lower storage cost and RAM
  • LogQL, a Prometheus-inspired query language for filtering and aggregating log streams
  • Native Grafana integration to explore, correlate and dashboard logs alongside metrics
  • Pluggable storage: local filesystem for small setups, S3/MinIO/GCS object storage to scale out
  • Configurable retention and compaction with a built-in compactor and per-tenant limits
  • Ingests from Promtail, Grafana Alloy, Fluentd, Fluent Bit and the Docker logging driver
Best for

Teams already using Grafana that want centralized, low-cost log aggregation for containers and servers without the operational weight of an Elasticsearch-based stack. Ideal as the logging backend of an observability stack: keep it on the internal network and feed it from Promtail or Grafana Alloy.

services: loki: image: grafana/loki:latest restart: unless-stopped command: -config.file=/etc/loki/local-config.yaml volumes: - ./loki_data:/loki - ./config/loki-config.yaml:/etc/loki/local-config.yaml:ro container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' expose: - 3100 networks: - internal healthcheck: test: - CMD - wget - -q - --spider - http://localhost:3100/ready interval: 30s timeout: 5s retries: 5 networks: internal: driver: bridge
Number of days Loki keeps ingested logs before the compactor deletes them.

# Grafana Loki configuration (single-binary, filesystem storage) # https://grafana.com/docs/loki/latest/configure/
auth_enabled: false
server: http_listen_port: 3100 grpc_listen_port: 9096 log_level: info
common: instance_addr: 127.0.0.1 path_prefix: /loki storage: filesystem: chunks_directory: /loki/chunks rules_directory: /loki/rules replication_factor: 1 ring: kvstore: store: inmemory
schema_config: configs: - from: 2020-10-24 store: tsdb object_store: filesystem schema: v13 index: prefix: index_ period: 24h
limits_config: retention_period: {{ retention_days }}d reject_old_samples: true reject_old_samples_max_age: 168h ingestion_rate_mb: 10 ingestion_burst_size_mb: 20
compactor: working_directory: /loki/compactor delete_request_store: filesystem retention_enabled: true retention_delete_delay: 2h compaction_interval: 10m
ruler: storage: type: local local: directory: /loki/rules rule_path: /loki/rules-temp enable_api: true
# For production scale, replace the filesystem backend above with S3/MinIO/GCS # object storage. Do not expose port 3100 publicly; query Loki from Grafana over # the internal network. analytics: reporting_enabled: false
